19.21: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Go down; testify among the people that they do not go too far before LORD JEHOVAH to see, or many of them will fall.
19.22: Also the Priests who come near to LORD JEHOVAH shall be hallowed, lest LORD JEHOVAH will overpower them."
19.23: And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH, “The people cannot ascend the mountain of Sinai because you testified with us and you said to me, “Set boundaries to the mountain and hallow it.”
19.24: And LORD JEHOVAH said to him, “Go, go down and come up, you and Ahron your brother with you, and do not let the Priests and people go far to go up before LORD JEHOVAH, lest he kill some of them.”
19.25: And Moshe came down to the people and spoke to them.

20.1: And God said all these words,
20.2: “I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God who brought you out from the land of Egypt from the house of bondage.
20.3: You shall not have other gods outside of me.
20.4: You shall not make for you any image or any form that is in Heaven from above or what is in the earth from beneath or what is in the water beneath the Earth:
20.5: You shall not bow to them and you shall not serve them because I AM LORD JEHOVAH your God, a jealous God; he pays the love debts of fathers for the children unto three and unto four generations for those hating me;
20.6: And I produce grace to thousands of generations of My friends and those keeping my commands.
20.7: You shall not swear in the Name of LORD JEHOVAH your God with a lie because LORD JEHOVAH does not declare him innocent who swears in his Name with a lie.
20.8: Remember the day of the Sabbath for its holiness.
20.9: Six days you shall serve and you shall do all your work:
20.10: And the seventh day is the Sabbath to LORD JEHOVAH your God; you shall not do any work, you or your son or your daughter or your Servant or your Maidservant or your beast or your settler who is in your town:
20.11: Because for six days LORD JEHOVAH made Heaven and Earth and the seas and everything that is in them, and he was refreshed in the seventh day; because of this, LORD JEHOVAH blessed the seventh day and hallowed it.
20.12: Honor your father and your mother that your days will increase in the land that LORD JEHOVAH your God gives to you.
20.16: You shall not testify a false testimony against your neighbor.
20.17: You shall not lust for the house of your neighbor; you shall not lust for the wife of your neighbor or his Servant or his Maid Servant or his ox or his donkey or anything that is your neighbor’s.
20.18: And all the people saw the voices and lamps lightnings and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, and the people saw and they were afraid and they stood at a distance.
20.19: And they said to Moshe, “You speak with us and we will hear and do not let God speak with us, lest we die.”
20.20: And Moshe said to the people, “You shall not be afraid, because God has come to test you and so that his awe may be before your faces and that you will not sin.”
20.21: And the people stood at a distance and Moshe came to the thick darkness of the place of God.
20.22: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “In this way speak to those of the house of Israel: “You have seen that I have spoken with you from Heaven.
20.23: You shall not make for yourselves gods of gold with me, neither gods of silver shall you make for yourselves.
20.24: An altar of earth you shall make to me and you will offer upon it your burnt offerings and your offerings and your sheep and your oxen; in every place that you will commemorate My Name I shall come to you and I shall bless you.
20.25: And if you make to me an altar of stone, you shall not build it with cut stone because you have lifted up iron upon it and you have polluted it.
20.26: And you shall not go up by stairs to my altar so that your nakedness will not be revealed upon it.

21.1: And these are the judgments that you shall set before them.
21.2: When you will buy a Hebrew Servant, six years he will serve you and in the seventh he will go forth as a son of freedom from your presence.
21.3: And if he alone will come in, he alone will go forth, and if he is a husband of a wife, his wife will go out with him.
21.4: And if his Master will give him a woman and she shall bear to him sons and daughters, the wife and her children will be his Master’s and he will go forth alone.
21.5: And if a Servant will say, “I Love my Master and my wife and my children; I am not going out a son of freedom”:
21.6: His Master shall bring him to the Judges and they shall bring him to the door or to the door post and his Master shall bore his ear with an awl and he will be his, doing service for life.
21.7: And when a man will sell his daughter as a Maidservant, she will not go out as Men Servants will leave.
21.8: If she is hated in the eyes of her Master so that he will not take her, let him redeem her for a foreign people; it is not legal for him to sell her, because he was false to her.
21.9: And if he will take her for his son, he shall do for her according to the Law of what is done to a daughter.
21.10: And if he will take to himself another wife; her provisions and her clothing and her bed he shall not take.
21.11: And if he will not do these three things to her, she will go out freely without expense.
21.12: He who strikes a man and the man dies, shall certainly be killed.
21.13: And he who did not lie in ambush for him and God handed him over into his hand, make for you a place that he may flee there.
21.14: And if a man will presume against his neighbor and will kill him by treachery, take him from the presence of my altar to kill him.
21.15: He who will strike his father or his mother shall certainly be killed.
21.16: He who will steal a person and will sell him and be found with him surely shall be killed.
21.17: He who will revile his father or his mother surely shall be killed.
21.18: And when two men will fight and a man will strike his neighbor with a stone or by force and he will not die and he will fall ill:
21.19: If he shall rise and he will walk in the street on his staff, he who hit him will be innocent, only he will give him his loss of work and the hire of a Physician.
21.20: And when a man will strike his Servant or his maid with a rod and one dies under his hand, he shall certainly be judged.
21.21: However, if one shall live one day or two days, he will not be judged because that is his money.
21.22: And when two men will fight and they strike a woman who is pregnant and her infant shall come out and there will be no misfortune, certainly he shall be fined according to what the husband of the woman lays upon him, and he will give as the Judges decide.
21.23: And if there is misfortune, he will give a life in exchange for a life,
21.24: An eye in exchange for an eye, a tooth in exchange for a tooth, a hand in exchange of a hand, a foot in exchange for a foot.
21.25: Branding in exchange for branding, wound in exchange for wound, a blow in exchange for a blow.
21.26: And when a man will strike the eye of his Servant or the eye of his Maidservant and will injure it, he will send him/her away a person of freedom for the sake of his/her eye.
21.27: And if he will knock out a tooth of his Servant or a tooth of his Maidservant, he will send that person away as a person of freedom for the sake of his tooth.
21.28: And when an ox will gore a man or a woman and someone will die, the ox surely will be stoned and its flesh shall not be eaten and the owner of the ox shall be declared innocent.
21.29: And if the ox was a gorer from yesterday or from before it and they testified before its owner and he did not keep it and it will kill a man or a woman, the ox will be stoned, also its owner will be killed.
21.30: And if they shall impose money upon him, he will give the redemption of his life as much as they ask him.
21.31: And if an ox will gore a son or a daughter, it will be done to it according to this judgment.
21.32: And if an ox will gore a Servant or a Maidservant, he shall give thirty shekels of silver to his/her Master and the ox shall be stoned.
21.33: And when a man will open a pit or a man will dig a well and will not cover them and an ox or a donkey will fall there;
21.34: The owner of the pit will pay money to its owner and the dead animal shall be his.
21.35: And when the ox of a man will gore the ox of another man, his neighbor, and it will die, they will sell the living ox and shall divide its money, also they shall divide that which is dead.
21.36: And if it will be known that an ox was a gorer from yesterday or from the day before yesterday, and its owner did not keep it in, he will give an ox in exchange for the ox and the dead ox shall be his.

22.1: And when a man will steal an ox or a ewe and he will kill them or he will sell them, he will give five oxen in exchange for an ox and four ewes he will give in exchange for a ewe.
22.2: And if a thief will be found breaking in and he is wounded and dies, there is no vengeance for him.
22.3: And if the sun has risen upon him there is vengeance for him, and paying he will pay, and if he has nothing, he shall be sold for his theft.
22.4: And if when he is caught, the theft of an ox or a donkey and unto a ewe shall be found in his hands still alive, he shall restore each by two.
22.5: And when a man will consume a field or a vineyard and will loose his cattle and will consume in another field, he shall pay from the good of his field and from the good of his vineyard.
22.6: And when fire goes forth and it will find the thorns and will devour the shocks or the standing grain or the field, he who kindled the fire shall certainly restore.
22.7: And when a man will give his neighbor money or a garment to keep and it is stolen from the house of the man, if the thief be found he will restore each thing double.
22.8: And if the thief will not be found, let the owner of the house approach to the Judge, if he did not reach his hand into the property of his neighbor
22.9: Concerning every matter of sin about an ox or about a donkey or about a sheep or about clothing or about every loss of which one says, “This is it” before the Judges, the words of both shall enter and he whom the Judges convict will restore each thing double to his neighbor.
22.10: And when a man will give his neighbor a donkey or an ox or a sheep or any animal to keep and it will die or will be injured or will be stolen and there is not a man who sees
22.11: An oath of LORD JEHOVAH will be between both: “Surely he did not stretch his hand into the property of his neighbor” and their owner would take the oaths and will not restore
22.12: And if it will be certainly stolen from him, he will restore to its owner.
22.13: And if it will be certainly injured and he shall bring it for a witness that it is injured, he will not restore it.
22.14: And when a man will ask an animal from his neighbor and it will be injured or it would die and its owner was not with it, he shall surely restore it.
22.15: And if its owner was with it, he shall not restore it, and if it was hired he will eat its hire.
22.16: And when a man will seduce a virgin that was not engaged and he shall have sexual intercourse with her, he shall surely take her to him as a wife.
22.17: And if her father does not choose to give her to him, he shall weigh out money as a marriage dowry of the virgin.
22.18: You shall not let a sorcerer live.
22.19: And everyone whoever will have sexual intercourse with an animal shall surely be killed.
22.20: He who will sacrifice to objects of worship, if not to LORD JEHOVAH alone, shall be destroyed.
22.21: And do not harm, neither distress settlers, because you were settlers in the land of Egypt.
22.22: And do not harm any widow or orphan.
22.23: And if you trouble them and they pray before me, I shall certainly hear their prayers;
22.24: And my anger will be kindled and I shall kill you with sword and your wives shall be widowed and your children shall be orphaned.
22.25: And if you lend money to my people, the poor of your people, you will not be as a creditor to him and do not take interest from him.
22.26: And if you take the garment of your neighbor as a pledge, at the setting the sun, give it to him:
22.27: Because that is his garment alone and it is his covering for his body. With what will he sleep? And if he will pray before me I shall hear him, because I am compassionate.
22.28: Do not vilify the Judge and do not curse the Ruler of your people.
22.29: You shall not delay the beginning of the harvests of your threshing floor or the first of your wine press; the firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.
22.30: And thus you shall do to your oxen and to your sheep: seven days he shall be with his mother, and on the eighth day you give him to me.
22.31: And you shall be holy people to me and the flesh that is torn apart by living animals you shall not eat; you shall cast it to the dogs.

23.1: You shall not confirm a false report; you shall not stretch your hand with the guilty to be a lying witness for him.
23.2: You shall not go after many to do evil and do not answer against judgment to turn away after many who do turn away:
23.3: And you shall not favor the poor in his judgment.
23.4: And when you will come upon the ox of your enemy or upon his donkey when going astray, be sure to return them to him.
23.5: And when you shall see the donkey of your enemy that is lying under his burden and you do not want to lift it up, surely lift it up.
23.6: You shall not decline the justice of the poor in his judgment.
23.7: And keep far away from a lying word, and you shall not kill the innocent ones and the righteous ones, because I do not justify the guilty.
23.8: Do not take a bribe because a bribe puts out the eyes of the wise in judgment and subverts the words of the innocent.
23.9: And do not distress foreigners, for you know the soul of foreigners, because you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
23.10: Six years you shall sow your land and you shall gather in its harvests:
23.11: And in the seventh you shall plough it and you shall leave it and the poor of your people shall eat and the animals of the wilderness will devour their remainder and thus you shall do to your vineyards and to your olive yards.
23.12: In six days you shall do your work and in the seventh day you shall rest, so that your ox and your donkey will rest, and the son of your maid Servant will be refreshed, and the stranger that is in your towns.
23.13: And pay attention to everything that I said to you and you shall not call to mind the name of other objects of worship and it shall not come up on your heart.
23.14: Make to me a feast three times in a year.
23.15: You shall keep the feast of unleavened bread seven days; you shall eat unleavened bread as I ordered you in the month of Habiba, because in the month of Habiba you went out from Egypt, and you shall not appear before me empty.
23.16: And the feast of beginning harvest of your crops that you will sow in a field, and the feast of ingathering in the outgoing of the year when you have gathered your increase from the field.
23.17: Three times in a year all your commemorations shall appear before LORD JEHOVAH your God.
23.18: You shall not offer blood of sacrifice with leaven and the fat of feasts will not remain until the morning.
23.19: The beginning of the harvests of your land you shall bring to the house of LORD JEHOVAH your God; you shall not boil a goat in the milk of its mother.
23.20: Behold, I am sending a Messenger before you, for he will keep you on the path and he will bring you to the land that I have prepared.
23.21: Beware of him and listen to his voice; do not contend against him, lest he will not forgive your sins, because my Name is upon him.
23.22: And if you will listen to hear his voice and you will do everything that he says to you, I shall hate those hating you and I shall distress your enemies.
23.23: Because My Messenger will go before you and he will bring you against the Amorites, and the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Canaanites, and the Khivites and the Yebusites, and I shall destroy them.
23.24: You shall not worship their gods and you shall not serve them, neither shall you do according to their works, but certainly cast them down and certainly break their monuments.
23.25: And you shall serve LORD JEHOVAH your God, and he shall bless your bread and your water, and plagues shall pass by from your houses.
23.26: And there will be no one sterile and no one barren in your land, and I shall fulfill the number of your days.
23.27: And I shall send my awe before you and I shall destroy all the nations where you go against them and I shall cause your enemies to turn their back before you.
23.28: And I shall send bees before you and I shall destroy the Canaanites and the Khethites from before you.
23.29: I shall not destroy them from before you in one year, that the land will not be a desolation, and wild animals multiply upon you.
23.30: Little by little I shall destroy them from before you, until you will prevail and you will inherit the land.
23.31: And I shall make your borders from the Sea of Reeds and unto the Sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness and unto the river, because I deliver the dwellers of the land into your hands and you will destroy them.
23.32: You shall not cause a covenant to stand with them and with their idols.
23.33: And they shall not dwell in your land, lest they cause you to sin before me, and you shall not serve their gods, that they will not be a stumbling block to you.

24.1: And to Moshe he said, “Come up to LORD JEHOVAH, you and Ahron and Nadab and Abihu and seventy men of the Elders of Israel, and you shall worship from a distance.
24.2: And Moshe alone will come near before LORD JEHOVAH, and they will not approach, and the people will not come up with him.
24.3: And Moshe came and he related to the people all of the statements of LORD JEHOVAH and all the judgments, and all the people cried out in one voice, and they said, “Everything that LORD JEHOVAH says, we shall do!”
24.4: And Moshe wrote all of the statements of LORD JEHOVAH, and he arose at dawn and built an altar on the outskirts of the mountain and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.
24.5: And he sent the youths of the children of Israel and they offered up burning peace offerings and they sacrificed peace sacrifices, bullocks to LORD JEHOVAH.
24.6: And Moshe took half of the blood and cast it into basins and half of the blood he poured on the altar.
24.7: And he took the Book of the Covenant and he read before the people and they said, “Everything that LORD JEHOVAH said, we will hear and we will do”
24.8: And Moshe took the blood and he sprinkled it on the people and said, “This is the blood of the Covenant that LORD JEHOVAH covenanted with you by all of these words.”
24.9: And Moshe and Ahron and Nadab and Abihu and seventy of the Elders of Israel came up:
24.10: And they saw the God of Israel, and under his feet there was as the work of tile of sapphire, and like the color of the sky in purity.
24.11: And upon the Elders of the children of Israel he did not stretch his hand, and they saw God, and they ate and they drank.
24.12: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Come up to me to the mountain and be there and I shall give to you tablets of stone and the laws and the commandments that I have written to teach them.”
24.13: And Moshe stood and Hoshea his Minister, and Moshe came up to the mountain of God.
24.14: And to the Elders he said, “Wait for us here until we return to you; and behold, Ahron and Khur are with you; he that has a matter, he will come before them.”
24.15: And Moshe came up to the mountain and a cloud covered it.
24.16: And the glory of LORD JEHOVAH dwelt on the mountain of Sinai and a cloud covered it six days, and LORD JEHOVAH called Moshe on the seventh day from within the cloud.
24.17: And he saw the glory of LORD JEHOVAH as fire that burned on the top of the mountain before the eyes of the entire house of Israel.
24.18: And Moshe entered within the cloud and he came up to the mountain and Moshe was in the mountain forty days and forty nights.

25.1: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him:
25.2: “Speak to them of the house of Israel and they will separate to me an offering from every man; what is considered in his heart you shall receive as an offering.
25.3: And this is the offering that you shall receive of them: gold and silver and brass,
25.4: And dark blue fringes, and purple, and scarlet, and fine white linen, and hair of goats,
25.5: And skin of rams that is dyed red, and skin of vermilion or sky blue, and wood of box tree,
25.6: And oil to give light, and fragrances to anoint for an anointing, and for incense of sweet spices,
25.7: And stones of Beryl, and precious stones for the ephod and for the breastplate.
25.8: And they will make to me a sanctuary and I shall dwell among them.
25.9: Everything that I show to you, the likeness of the Tabernacle and of all its implements, in this way they shall do.
25.10: And they shall make an ark of the wood of the box tree, two cubits and a half its length, and a cubit and a half its width, and a cubit and a half its height.
25.11: And overlay it with pure gold on the outside, and from within you shall overlay it, and you shall make for it a crown of gold encircling.
25.12: And you shall forge for it four rings of gold and you shall put on the four corners, two rings on one side and two rings on its other side.
25.13: And you shall make poles of the wood of the box tree and you shall overlay them in gold.
25.14: And you shall put the poles in the rings on the side of the ark by which the ark will be carried.
25.15: The poles shall be in the rings of the ark and they shall not be taken away from them.
25.16: And you will put in the ark the testimony that I shall give to you.
25.17: And make the Mercy Seat of pure gold, two cubits and a half its length and a cubit and a half its width.
25.18: And make two Cherubim of gold; make them of smelting from the two sides of the Mercy Seat.
25.19: And make one Cherub at its side from here and another Cherub at the other side of the Mercy Seat; you shall make two Cherubim on its two sides.
25.20: And the Cherubim shall be extending their wings over and covering over the Mercy Seat with their wings, and their faces one against another; the faces of the Cherubim will be over the Mercy Seat.
25.21: And you will put the Mercy Seat on the ark from above and you will put in the ark the testimony that I shall give to you.
25.22: And I shall have a meeting with you there, and I shall speak with you from over the Mercy Seat from between the two Cherubim that are over the Ark of the Testimony, everything that I command you about the children of Israel.
25.23: And make a table of the wood of the box tree, two cubits its length, and a cubit its width, and a cubit and a half its height.
25.24: And overlay it in pure gold and make for it a crown of gold encircling.
25.25: And make for it a rim that may be a handbreadth high encircling and make a crown of gold encircling on the rim.
25.26: And make for it four rings of gold and you shall make four rings of gold on its four corners over its four feet.
25.27: Against its rim its rings shall be a place for the poles to carry the table.
25.28: And make poles of wood of the box tree and overlay them in gold, by which the table is to be carried.
25.29: And make dishes and ladles and vessels and bowls into which things are poured; of pure gold you shall make them.
25.30: And you will put on the table the bread of The Faces before me constantly.
25.31: And make a lamp stand of pure gold; you shall make the menorah of smelting; its base and its branches and its bowls and its apples and its lilies shall be of it.
25.32: And six branches that will come out from its sides: three branches from one side of the menorah and three branches from its other side:
25.33: Three bowls are joined on one branch and the apples and lilies and three bowls joined on another branch, and the apples and the lilies in this way on six branches will come out from the lamp stand.
25.34: And on the Menorah shall be four bowls and apples and lilies.
25.35: An apple under two branches of it, and an apple under another two branches of it, and an apple under another two branches of it, so it shall be with the six branches that come out from the lamp stand.
25.36: And the apples and the branches of it shall be all of one smelting of pure gold.
25.37: And make its seven lamps and light its lamps that they will be giving light against its face.
25.38: And make its snuffers and its bases of pure gold.
25.39: From a talent of pure gold you shall make it and all these implements.
25.40: And see and make it in this likeness that I show to you in the mountain.

26.1: And for the Tabernacle you shall make ten curtains of fine woven white linen, blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet; you shall make Cherubim, the work of a craftsman.
26.2: The length of one curtain is twenty and eight cubits, and its width, four cubits; all curtains have the one measure of the one curtain.
26.3: Five curtains will be joined one to one, and five curtains will be joined one to one.
26.4: And make curtain rings of blue fringes on the edge of one curtain that are joined on its side, and thus you shall make on the side of the curtain from the other side that is joined.
26.5: Fifty rings you shall make on the edge of one curtain and fifty rings you shall make on the other edge of the curtain in the other coupling, and they shall be upright rings, one against one.
26.6: And make fifty clasps of gold and join one curtain on another with a clasp, and the Tabernacle will be one.
26.7: And make a curtain of hair for a covering of the Tabernacle; eleven curtains you shall make them.
26.8: The length of one curtain of them, thirty cubits, and its width four cubits, the one measure of one curtain is for all these eleven curtains.
26.9: And join five curtains as one, and six curtains as one, and fold the sixth curtain against the face of the Tabernacle.
26.10: And make fifty rings on the edge of one curtain from the side that is joined and fifty rings on the edge of a curtain in joining another.
26.11: And make fifty clasps of brass and bring the clasps into the rings and join the Tabernacle and it will be one.
26.12: And what is left of the curtains of the Tabernacle, half of a curtain remaining, you shall hang to the back of the Tabernacle.
26.13: And a cubit from one side and a cubit from the other side remaining in the length of the curtains of the Tabernacle shall be hanging over the side of the Tabernacle, from this side and from that, for its covering.
26.14: And make a cover for the Tabernacle, skins of rams that are dyed red, skins of rams of sky blue from above.
26.15: And make boards for the Tabernacle of the wood of box tree standing.
26.16: Ten cubits the length of one board and a cubit and half the width of one board.
26.17: And two sockets to one upright board, one against one, in this way you shall make all the boards of the Tabernacle.
26.18: And make the boards for the Tabernacle, twenty boards for the side of the wind of the south.
26.19: And forty sockets of silver you shall make under twenty boards, two sockets under one board for its two hinges and two sockets under another board for its two hinges.
26.20: And for the other side of the Tabernacle from the wind of the north, twenty boards:
26.21: And forty sockets of silver, two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
26.22: And from the outside of the Tabernacle from the west make six boards.
26.23: And two boards you shall make for the corners of the Tabernacle from their outer sides.
26.24: That they will be equal from the bottom and they shall be coupled together on its top on one ring handle, in this way to both sockets, they shall be for two corners.
26.25: And there shall be eight boards and their sixteen sockets of silver, two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
26.26: And make the bars from the wood of the box tree, five for the boards of one side of the Tabernacle,
26.27: And five bars for the boards of the other side of the Tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the other side at the outside of the Tabernacle on the west.
26.28: And pass the middle bars between the boards from this side to that.
26.29: And overlay the boards in gold, and make the rings of gold the place for the bars and overlay the bars in gold.
26.30: And erect the Tabernacle rightly as I showed you in the mountain.
26.31: And make a covering of fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen; make a Cherub, the work of a craftsman:
26.32: And place it on four pillars of boxwood overlaid in gold and their nails of gold on four sockets of silver.
26.33: And put a covering under the boards and bring there the ark of the Testimony inside the covering and you shall spread out for you a covering between the Holy and between The Holy of Holies.
26.34: And you shall put the Mercy Seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Holy of Holies.
26.35: And you shall put the table outside of the covering and the menorahs opposite the table on the south side of the Tabernacle, and the table you shall put on the north side.
26.36: And make a covering for the door of the Tabernacle, the blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen, the work of embroidery.
26.37: And make for the coverings five pillars of boxwood and overlay them in gold and their nails of gold and make for them five sockets of brass.

27.1: And make the altar of the wood of the box tree, five cubits its length and five cubits its width; the altar shall be square and its height three cubits.
27.2: And make its horns; its horns will be on the four corners of it, and overlay it with brass.
27.3: And make its pots for its ministry and its cauldrons, and its hanging pots, and its forks, and its censers, and all its implements make of brass.
27.4: And make a grate as the work of a network of brass and make four rings of brass over the grate, over its four sides.
27.5: Put it under the outside of the altar underneath, and the grate shall be unto the middle of the altar.
27.6: And make poles for the altar, poles of the wood of box tree and overlay them with brass.
27.7: And put poles in the rings and they shall be on two sides of the altar when they carry it.
27.8: Hollow with boards you shall make it, as I showed you in the mountain, in this way they shall do.
27.9: And make a courtyard for the Tabernacle on the side of the south wind, the curtain of the courtyard of fine woven white linen, a hundred cubits the length of one curtain.
27.10: Its pillars are twenty and their sockets of brass twenty, and the nails of the pillars and their overlay of silver.
27.11: And thus for the side of the north, a curtain of a hundred cubits in length, and its pillars twenty, and their twenty sockets of brass, and the nails of the pillars and their overlays of silver.
27.12: And the width of the courtyard to the west side is fifty cubits, their pillars, ten, and their sockets, ten.
27.13: And the width of the courtyard to the side of the wind of the east, fifty cubits.
27.14: And fifteen cubits a curtain of the side; their pillars, three, and their sockets, three.
27.15: And for the second side fifteen curtains, their pillars, three, and their sockets, three.
27.16: And for the gate of the courtyard a covering of twenty cubits, blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen, work of embroidery; their pillars, four, and their sockets, four.
27.17: All of the encircling pillars of the courtyard, overlaid with silver, and their nails of silver, and their sockets of brass.
27.18: And the length of the courtyard a hundred cubits, and its width, fifty each, and its height, five cubits of fine woven white linen, and their sockets of brass.
27.19: All of the implements of the Tabernacle and all its service and all its poles and all the stakes of the courtyard are of brass.
27.20: And you command the children of Israel and take to you pure oil of trodden olives to light the lamps constantly
27.21: In the Time Tabernacle outside of the covering that is upon the Testimony, and Ahron and his sons will set them in order from evening and unto the morning before LORD JEHOVAH: the law to eternity for your generations from the presence of the children of Israel.

28.1: And you bring to yourselves Ahron your brother and his children with him from within the children of Israel to serve as Priest to me, Ahron and Nadab and Abihu and Eliazar and Ithamar, the sons of Ahron.
28.2: And make holy garments for Ahron your brother for honor and for glory.
28.3: And you say to all the wise of heart whom the Spirit of Wisdom has filled and they shall make holy garments for Ahron to hallow him to serve as Priest to me.
28.4: And these are the garments that they shall make for them: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a cloak, and a coat of fine white linen, and a turban, and a girdle; and they shall make holy garments for Ahron your brother and for his sons to serve as Priests to me.
28.5: And those shall take gold and blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine white linen.
28.6: And they shall make the ephod of gold, the blue fringes, and purple, and dye of scarlet, and fine woven white linen, work of a craftsman.
28.7: Let two shoulders be joined to it on its two sides and it shall join.
28.8: The girdle of the ephod on him: the work of it shall be as of gold and blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen.
28.9: And take two stones of beryl and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel
28.10: Six of their names engrave on one stone and the six names that remain engrave on the other stone, according to their births.
28.11: Engrave the work of a craftsman of stone, the engraving of a seal; engrave two stones with the names of the sons of Israel; make them mounted and set in gold.
28.12: And set two stones on the shoulder of the ephod, stones of memorial to the children of Israel, and Ahron will be bearing their names before LORD JEHOVAH on his two shoulders for a memorial.
28.14: And two chains of pure gold, make them double twined work, and set the two chains double on the settings.
28.15: And make the breastplate of judgment the work of a craftsman, as the work of the ephod; make it gold and blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and make it fine woven white linen.
28.16: It shall be square when double, a span its length and a span its width.
28.17: And you shall set in it settings of stones, four rows of stone: the first row, ruby and topaz and lightning (diamond), row one.
28.18: And another row, a garnet and sapphire and an onyx.
28.19: And the third row, a jacinth and a chalcedony and a calf’s eye.
28.20: And the fourth row, chrysolith and beryl and jasper, and they will be mounted, set in gold in their settings.
28.21: And let the stones be engraved with the names of the twelve sons of Israel by their names, the engraving of a seal; let each man be according to his name for the twelve tribes.
28.22: And make double chains on the breastplate, twined work of pure gold.
28.23: And make two clasps of pure gold for the Mercy Seat and put two clasps on the two sides of the breastplate.
28.24: And put two braids of gold on two clasps upon the two sides of the breastplate.
28.25: And the two braids put on two settings and you shall put it on the shoulders of the ephod on its front.
28.26: And make two clasps of gold and set them on the two sides of the breastplate on the side that is upon the edge of the ephod from within.
28.27: And make two clasps of gold and set them on the two shoulders of the ephod from beneath, on its front, against its joint, above the girdle of the ephod.
28.28: And they shall join the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod, with a cord of blue fringe to be on the girdle of the ephod, that it will not remove the breastplate from the ephod.
28.29: And Ahron will be bearing the names of the sons of Israel in the breastplate of judgment on his breast when he enters the Holiness, a memorial before LORD JEHOVAH constantly.
28.30: And put on the breastplate of judgment, light and peace, and they shall be on the breast when he enters before LORD JEHOVAH, and Ahron will bear the judgment of the children of Israel on the breast before LORD JEHOVAH constantly.
28.31: And make the apron of the ephod entirely of blue.
28.32: And the opening of its top will have within it a lip; it will be for the opening encircling the work, woven like a mouth; it shall be of cord that it will not rip.
28.33: And make on its hem, pomegranates of blue and purple, and dye of scarlet on its hem, encircling, and bells of gold encircling between them:
28.34: And a bell, a bell of gold and pomegranates on the hem of the apron, encircling.
28.35: And it will be on Ahron for ministration, and its sound will be heard when he enters the Holiness before LORD JEHOVAH, and whenever he exits, so that he would not die.
28.36: And make a crown of pure gold and engrave upon it an engraving of the seal: "Holiness to LORD JEHOVAH.”
28.37: And put it on a cord of the blue fringes and it shall be on the turban, and it shall be on the front of the turban.
28.38: And it shall be over and between the eyes of Ahron, and Ahron shall be carrying the sins of the holy things which the children of Israel hallow for all the gifts of their holy things, and it shall be over between his eyes constantly, for their acceptance before LORD JEHOVAH.
28.39: And make a coat of fine white linen and make a turban of fine white linen and make the girdle the work of an artist.
28.40: And make coats for the children of Ahron, and make for them girdles and tiaras; you make them for honor and for glory.
28.41: And you shall clothe them, Ahron your brother and his children with him, and anoint them and perfect their hands, and hallow them, and they shall serve as Priests to me.
28.42: And make aprons of fine white linen for them to cover the flesh of their nakedness; they shall be from their waist and unto their thighs:
28.43: And they shall be on Ahron and upon his sons when they enter the Time Tabernacle, and whenever they come to the altar to serve in the Holy Place, so that they will not receive sins and die; the Written Law to eternity for him and for his seed after him.

29.1: And thus make for them and hallow them to me for the priesthood and take one bull, son of an ox, and two rams without blemish,
29.2: And the unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil and unleavened cakes mixed with oil, you shall make them of ground wheat.
29.3: And you shall put them in one basket and you shall bring them in a basket, and the bull and two rams.
29.4: And bring Ahron and his sons to the door of the Time Tabernacle and wash them in water.
29.5: And take the garments and clothe Ahron with the coat and the apron of the ephod, and turban, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and bestow him with the girdle of the ephod:
29.6: And put the turban on his head and put the crown of Holiness on the turban.
29.7: And take the oil of anointing and pour it on his head and anoint him.
29.8: And bring his sons and clothe them with the coats.
29.9: And bind girdles to Ahron and to his sons and put tiaras on them, and their priesthood shall be to them for an eternal covenant, and you shall consecrate the hand of Ahron and the hands of his sons.
29.10: And bring a bull before the Time Tabernacle and Ahron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.
29.11: And slaughter the bull in front of LORD JEHOVAH in the doorway of the Time Tent.
29.12: And take of the blood of the bull and sprinkle it on the horns of the altar with your finger and pour out all the blood on the base of the altar.
29.13: And take all the fat that covers the entrails and the liver caul and two kidneys and their fat and offer them up on the altar.
29.14: And the flesh of the ox and his skin and his dung you shall burn in fire outside of the camp; it is sin.
29.15: Take one ram, and Ahron and his sons will lay their hands on the head of the ram.
29.16: And slaughter the ram and take some of his blood and sprinkle it on the altar as a circle.
29.17: And cut in pieces the ram’s limbs and wash his insides and his legs and you shall lay them on his limbs and upon his head.
29.18: And offer up the whole ram on the altar; it is a sacrifice to LORD JEHOVAH for a sweet savor offering to LORD JEHOVAH.
29.19: And take the second ram, and Ahron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.
29.20: And slaughter the ram and take from his blood and sprinkle it on the lobe of the right ear of Ahron, and upon the lobes of the right ears of his sons, and upon the thumbs of their right hands, and upon the big toes of their right feet, and sprinkle the blood on the altar as a circle.
29.21: And take from the blood upon the altar and from the oil of anointing and sprinkle it on Ahron and upon his garment and upon his sons and upon the clothing of his sons with him, and he will be hallowed, and his clothes and his sons and the clothes of his sons with him.
29.22: And take from the ram the fat and the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails and the liver caul and two kidneys and the fat that is upon them and the right shoulder, because that ram is of the consummation
29.23: And one loaf of bread, and one loaf of bread of oil, and one cake from the basket of unleavened bread that is before LORD JEHOVAH
29.24: And you will put all of them on the hands of Ahron and upon the hands of his sons and you shall set them apart as a portion before LORD JEHOVAH.
29.25: And you shall receive from their hands and you shall offer the breast of the ram on the altar for a pleasant savor sacrifice before LORD JEHOVAH; it is a gift to LORD JEHOVAH.
29.26: And you shall take the breast of the ram of the consecration of Ahron and you shall separate it as a portion set apart before LORD JEHOVAH, and there shall be a portion for you.
29.27: And hallow the breast of the set apart portion and the shank of the set apart portion which is appointed and is offered up from the ram of the consecration of Ahron and of his sons:
29.28: And it will be a covenant to eternity for Ahron and for his sons from the presence of the children of Israel, because that is a set apart portion and shall be a set apart portion from the children of Israel, from the sacrifices of their peace offering sacrifices, a gift to LORD JEHOVAH.
29.29: And the holy garments of Ahron shall be for his sons after him to be anointed in them and their hands are to be consecrated in them.
29.30: Seven days the Priest will wear them; after him, one of his sons, that he will enter the Time Tent to serve in Holiness.
29.31: And take the ram of the consummation and boil his flesh in the hallowed place.
29.32: And Ahron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket in the door of the Time Tent.
29.33: And they that atoned for them shall eat them to perfect their hands and to hallow them, and the foreigner shall not eat because they are holy.
29.34: And if some of the flesh of consummation and of the bread will remain until the morning, burn anything that remains with fire and they shall not be eaten, because they are Holiness.
29.35: And do for Ahron and his children in this way, just as I ordered you; you shall consecrate their hands for seven days.
29.36: And a bull of sin you shall serve in the day of atonement and you shall sprinkle on the altar when you make atonement upon it and you shall anoint it to sanctify it.
29.37: Seven days you will make atonement on the altar and you shall sanctify it and the altar shall be The Holy of Holies, and everything that will touch the altar will be made holy.
29.38: And thus you shall offer lambs on the altar, sons of two years, in the daytime constantly.
29.39: One lamb offer at dawn and another lamb you shall offer at evening time:
29.40: And a tenth part of fine flour that is sprinkled with a fourth of a hin of pressed oil and a drink offering a fourth of a hin of wine for the one lamb.
29.41: And the second lamb you shall offer at evening time, like the offering of dawn and like its drink offering; you will make it for a sweet savor offering to LORD JEHOVAH.
29.42: A sacrifice constantly for your generations in the door of the Time Tent before LORD JEHOVAH where I shall meet you there to speak with you.
29.43: And I shall meet there the children of Israel and I shall be sanctified in my honor.
29.44: And I shall hallow the Time Tabernacle and the altar and Ahron and his sons, and I shall hallow for you the Priesthood.
29.45: And I shall dwell within the children of Israel and I shall be to them God.
29.46: And I shall dwell among them; I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God, and they shall know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH their God, for I have brought them out from the land of Egypt.

30.1: And make an altar of incense offering; you shall make it of the wood of the box tree.
30.2: A cubit its length, a cubit its width; it shall be square, and its height two cubits; its horns shall be part of it.
30.3: And overlay it in pure gold, its top and its walls all around and its horns, and make it a crown of gold like a circle.
30.4: And you shall make two rings of gold for it under its crown, on the two corners you shall make on its two sides, and they will be the places for the poles to carry it by them.
30.5: And make the poles of wood of the box tree and overlay them in gold.
30.6: And place it before the covering that is over the Ark of the Testimony before the Mercy Seat that is over the Testimony, and I shall meet you there.
30.7: And Ahron will be burning incense of sweet spices upon it from dawn to evening; whenever he prepares the lamp he will burn it.
30.8: And whenever Ahron lights a lamp between suns, he shall burn incense constantly before LORD JEHOVAH for your generations.
30.9: You shall not offer up foreign incense upon it, and sacrifice and offering and drink offering you shall not pour upon it.
30.10: And Ahron shall make atonement on the horns of the altar once a year from the blood of sin offering of the atonement; once in a year he shall make atonement upon it for your generations; it is The Holy of Holies to LORD JEHOVAH.”
30.11: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him,
30.12: “When you will receive a number of the children of Israel, for their number each man shall give the redemption of his soul to LORD JEHOVAH whenever you have numbered them, that no death shall be among them whenever you have numbered them.
30.13: In this way all who are present in the number shall give a half shekel in a holy shekel, twenty
30.14: /4 shekels to a shekel; a half shekel is the offering to LORD JEHOVAH.
30.15: All who are present in the number from a son of twenty years and above will give an offering to LORD JEHOVAH.
30.16: He who is rich will not increase and he who is poor will not diminish from a half a shekel, to give an offering to LORD JEHOVAH to atone for your souls.
30.17: And you shall take the money of atonement from the children of Israel and you shall give to the service of the Time Tabernacle and it will be for the children of Israel as a remembrance before LORD JEHOVAH to atone for their souls.”
30.18: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and he said to him,
30.19: “Make a basin of brass and its base of brass for washing and set it between the Time Tabernacle and the altar and put water there.
30.20: And Ahron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet from it:
30.21: Whenever they enter the Time Tabernacle they shall wash with water and they will not die, and whenever they come near the altar to serve and to burn an incense offering to LORD JEHOVAH:
30.22: They shall wash their hands and their feet that they will not die and there shall be for them an eternal covenant, to him and to his seed for their generations.”
30.23: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him,
30.24: “You take to yourselves excellent incense, pure myrrh,five hundred shekels, and sweet cinnamon, half of it two hundred and fifty shekels, and a rod of sweet incense, two hundred and fifty shekels,
30.25: And cassia, five hundred holy shekels and oil of olives, a hin:
30.26: And make holy anointing oil, make fragrant incense, fragrant oil of anointing and it shall be holiness.
30.27: And you shall anoint the Time Tabernacle and the Ark of the Testimony with it,
30.28: And the table and all its implements and the Menorah and its implements and the altar of incense,
30.29: And the altar of burnt offering and all its implements and the basin and its base.
30.30: And hallow them and they shall be The Holy of Holies, and everything that will touch them will be hallowed.
30.31: And Ahron and his children you shall anoint and you shall hallow them to serve as Priests to me.
30.32: And speak with those of the house of Israel and say, ‘The oil of the anointing for holiness, this shall be mine for your generations.
30.33: It shall not be rubbed on the flesh of man and you will not make any in its likeness because it is holiness, and holiness it shall be to you.
30.34: And a man who will make fragrance like it and he who will give of it to a foreigner will be destroyed from his people.’”
30.35: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Take to yourselves sweet spices: oil of myrrh and onycha and fragrant resin incense and frankincense; it shall be pure and balanced:
30.36: And make it incense of ointment, fragrant work mixed that is purified for holiness:
30.37: And pound some of it and break it up and give some of it before the Testimony of the Time Tabernacle where I shall meet with you there; The Holy of Holies it shall be to you.
30.38: And the incense that you will make, you will not make in its likeness for yourselves; it shall be holiness to you for LORD JEHOVAH.

31.1: And a man who will make any like it to smell it will be destroyed from his people.
31.2: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe and said to him,
31.3: “See, I called by name Batsliel, son of Uri, son of Khur, from the tribe of Yehuda:
31.4: And the Spirit of God has filled him with wisdom and with understanding and with knowledge and with all service,
31.5: To teach workmanship to work in gold and in silver and in brass,
31.6: And to finish with craft of stone and with carving of wood to make every work.
31.7: And I, behold, I have given with him Elihab, son of Akhismak, from the tribe of Dan and I have put into the heart of all the wise ones a heart of wisdom and to do everything that I ordered you;
31.8: The Time Tabernacle and the Ark of the Testimony and the Mercy Seat that is upon it and all the implements of the Tabernacle,
31.9: And the table and all its implements and the pure menorah and all its instruments and the altar of incense,
31.10: And the altar of burnt offering and all its vessels and the basin and its base,
31.11: And the garments of the ministry and the holy garments for Ahron the Priest and the garments for his sons to serve as Priests to me,
31.12: And the oil of the anointing and the incense of sweet spices for holiness; everything that I ordered you they shall do.”
31.13: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe:
31.14: “You say to the children of Israel, ‘Be keeping the Sabbath because that is a sign between me and you for your generations, that you will know that I AM LORD JEHOVAH, I Who hallow you.
31.15: Keep the Sabbath because that is Holiness to you; everyone who weakens it shall surely be killed, and everyone who will do work in it, that man shall be destroyed from his people.
31.16: Six days you will do works and on the seventh day is the Sabbath rest; it is holiness to LORD JEHOVAH; everyone who will do work on the day of the Sabbath certainly shall be killed.
31.17: And the children of Israel shall keep the Sabbath to LORD JEHOVAH to make the Sabbath for their descendants a covenant for eternity.
31.18: Between me and the house of Israel it is a sign to eternity because for six days LORD JEHOVAH made the Heavens and Earth and the seas and everything that is in them and in the seventh day He ceased and was refreshed.’”

32.1: And he gave to Moshe when He had finished speaking with him in the mountain of Sinai, two tablets of the Testimony, tablets of stone that are written with the finger of God.
32.2: And the people saw that Moshe delayed to come down from the mountain and the people assembled to Ahron and they were saying to him, “Arise, make for us gods to go before us, because this man Moshe who brought us up from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.”
32.3: Ahron said to them, “Remove the earrings of gold that are in the ears of your wives and of your sons and of your daughters, and bring to me.”
32.4: And all the people removed the rings that were in their ears and they brought them to Ahron.
32.5: And he took it from them and fashioned it in a figure and he made it a calf that was molten and they said, “This is your God, Israel, that brought you up from the land of Egypt.”
32.6: And Ahron was afraid and built an altar before it and Ahron called and said, “There is a feast for LORD JEHOVAH tomorrow.”
32.7: And they arose the day after it and they offered sacrifices and they sacrificed peace offerings and they brought offerings and the people sat to eat and to drink and they arose to be merry and to play.
32.8: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Go down, go from here because your people is corrupt, whom you brought out from Egypt:
32.9: They have gone astray quickly from the way that I have commanded them and have made for themselves a molten calf and worshipped it, and they sacrificed to it and they said, ‘This is your God, Israel, that brought you up from the land of Egypt.’”
32.10: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “I have seen this people and behold the people is hard necked:
32.11: Now leave me and my anger will prevail with them and I shall destroy them and I shall make you a great nation.”
32.12: And Moshe prayed before LORD JEHOVAH his God and said, “No LORD JEHOVAH! Your wrath will not prevail with your people whom you brought out from Egypt by your great power and by your high arm.
32.13: And the Egyptians shall not say, ‘For their harm He brought out them to kill them among the mountains and to destroy them from the face of the Earth.’ Rest from the heat of your wrath and be reconciled to the evil of your people.
32.14: Remember Abraham and Isaac and Israel your Servants, for you swore to them by yourself and you said to them, ‘I shall multiply your seed as the stars that are in Heaven and all the land which I said I shall give to your seed and they shall inherit it to eternity.’
32.15: And LORD JEHOVAH came to an agreement concerning the evil that He said to do to his people.
32.16: And Moshe turned and came down from the mountain and two tablets of the Testimony were in his hand, tablets that were inscribed from both their sides, from one and to the other they were written.
32.17: And the tablets were the work of God and the writing was the writing of God; it was present on the tablets.
32.18: And Yeshua heard the sound of people when fighting and he said to Moshe, “The sound of war is in the camp.”
32.19: Moshe said to him, “It is not the voice of the speech of mighty men, neither the voice of misfortune but the sound of sin I heard.”
32.20: And when he came to the encampment he saw the calf and castanets and Moshe was angered, and he cast the tablets from his hand and he shattered them at the base of the mountain.
32.21: And he took the calf that they had made and he burned it in fire and he filed it with a file until it was pulverized like dust and he scattered it; he ground it on the face of the waters and he gave it to those of the house of Israel to drink.
32.22: And Moshe said to Ahron, “What did this people do to you that you brought upon it great sin?”
32.23: And Ahron said, “Let not the wrath of my Lord be hot; you know this people is evil.
32.24: And they said to me, ‘Make for us gods to go before us, because this Moshe who brought us up from the land of Egypt, we do not know what happened to him.’
32.25: And I said to them, ‘He that has gold, bring it to me’, and they brought it to me and I laid it in fire and it became this calf.”
32.26: And Moshe saw the people that they had sinned against themselves, for Ahron caused them to sin, that their names would be stinking at their end.
32.27: And Moshe stood himself in the gate of the Tabernacle and he said, “Who is from LORD JEHOVAH? He will come to me.” And all of the children of Levi assembled to him.
32.28: And Moshe said to them, “Thus says LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel, ‘Throw a man his sword around his waist and pass through and turn from door to door in the camp, and kill each man his brother, and each man his neighbor, and each man his relative.”
32.29: And the sons of Levi did according to the word of Moshe, and some of the people fell in that day, about three thousand men.
32.30: Moshe said to them, “Fill your hands today for LORD JEHOVAH, a man with his son and with his brother, because today a blessing will come upon you.”
32.31: And it was the day after it and Moshe said to the people, “You have sinned this great sin; now I shall ascend to LORD JEHOVAH; perhaps He shall forgive your debts.”
32.32: And Moshe returned to LORD JEHOVAH and said, “I beg you LORD JEHOVAH God, truly this people has sinned a great sin and they have made for themselves gods of gold.
32.33: Now perhaps you will forgive their sins, and if not, obliterate me from your book that you have written.”
32.34: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, the one who sins against me I shall obliterate from my book.
32.35: Now go, bring this people to where I told you and behold, My Messenger shall go before you, and in the day of my visitation I shall visit upon them their sins.”

33.1: And LORD JEHOVAH struck the people because they worshiped the calf that Ahron had made.
33.2: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Come up; go from here, you and your people whom you brought up from Egypt, to the land that I swore to Abraham and Isaac and Yaquuv and I said to them, ‘To your seed I shall give it.’
33.3: And I shall send before you the Messenger and he shall destroy the Canaanites and the Amorites and the Khithites and the Perizites and the Khivites and the Yebusites.
33.4: To the land that flows milk and honey, because I am not going up among you, because you are a people of hard necks, lest I shall destroy you in the way.”
33.5: And the people heard this evil response and it grieved them and no man took up his weapons upon him.
33.6: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Say to the children of Israel, ‘You people are hard necks; one hour I shall go up among you and I shall destroy you; now put aside your armor from you and I shall know what I shall do to you.’
33.7: And the children of Israel took their armor from them at the mountain of Khorib.
33.8: And Moshe took his tent and pitched it for him outside of the camp and removed it far from the camp and he called it the Time Tent, and everyone who did inquire from LORD JEHOVAH went out to the Time Tent outside of the camp.
33.9: And whenever Moshe went out to the Tent, all the people stood and were presented, each person, at the door of his tent and looked at the back of Moshe until he entered the tent.
33.10: And when Moshe entered the tent, the pillar of cloud came down and stood at the door of the tent and spoke with Moshe.
33.11: And all the people saw the pillar of cloud that stood in the doorway of the Tabernacle and all the people stood and they worshipped, each person in the door of his tent.
33.12: And LORD JEHOVAH spoke with Moshe face to face, as a man who speaks with his neighbor, and he returned to the encampment and his Attendant, Hosha Bar Nun, a boy, was not departing from within the Tabernacle.
33.13: And Moshe said to LORD JEHOVAH, “See, you have said to me, ‘Bring up this people’, and you have not shown me whom you are sending with me, and you have said: ‘I have known you by name, also you have found love in my eyes.’
33.14: Now, if I have found love in your eyes, show me your path and I shall know you, because I shall find love in your eyes, and see that your people, this people is great.
33.15: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Go before me, and I shall give rest to you.”
33.16: He said to him, “If you are not going with us, you will not bring us up from here.
33.17: And in what will it be known here that I have found love in your eyes, I and your people, but when you have gone with us and we are distinguished, I and your people, from all of the nations that are on the face of the earth?”
33.18: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “According to this word that you said, I shall do, because you have found love in my eyes, and I shall know you by name.”
33.19: And he said, “Show me your glory.”
33.20: And he said, “I shall make all my goodness pass before you and I shall call out the Name LORD JEHOVAH before you and I shall take pity on him on whom I shall take pity and I shall love him whom I shall love.”
33.21: And he said, “You cannot see my face, because no son of man sees me and lives.”
33.22: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Behold the place before me; stand on the flint.
33.23: And as my glory passes by, I shall put you in the cave of flint and I shall rest my hand over you until I shall pass:

34.1: And I shall pass over my hands and you shall see my back, and my faces shall not be seen.
34.2: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe: “Hew for you two tablets of stone like the former, and I shall write on the tablets the statements that were on the former tablets that you broke.
34.3: And be ready at dawn and come up at dawn to the mountain of Sinai and stand there at the top the mountain.
34.4: And a man shall not ascend with you and a man shall not appear in the whole mountain, neither a flock nor oxen shall feed against that mountain.
34.5: And he hewed two tablets of stone like the former and Moshe arose at dawn and came up to the mountain of Sinai just as LORD JEHOVAH commanded him and he took the two tablets of stone in his hand.
34.6: And LORD JEHOVAH came down in a cloud and stood with him there and he called out the Name of LORD JEHOVAH.
34.7: And LORD JEHOVAH passed by before him and called out LORD JEHOVAH, LORD JEHOVAH God, compassionate and brooding, he that is patient in his Spirit and abundant in his grace and in his truth,
34.8: He keeps grace for thousands of generations and forgives sins and debts and he does not declare innocent to declare innocent, visiting the debts of the fathers on the children and on the children of children, on three and upon four generations.
34.9: And Moshe made haste and he fell on the ground and he bowed.
34.10: And said, “If I have found love in your eyes, my Lord, let now my Lord go with us, because the people are hard necks, and forgive our debts and our sins and our conscience.”
34.11: And he said, “Behold, I establish a covenant before all your people and I shall make distinctions that not have been made in all the Earth and in all the nations, and all this people shall see that you within them are the Servant of LORD JEHOVAH, for what I do with you is fearsome.
34.12: Keep the thing that I command you today; behold I destroy from before you the Canaanites and Amorites and Khithites and Perizzites and Khivites and Yebusites.
34.13: Beware that you will not establish a covenant with those inhabiting the land where you go, lest it shall be a stumbling block to you.
34.14: Break down their altars and break their monuments and cut down their idols:
34.15: You shall not worship another god, because LORD JEHOVAH is zealous; the name of God is zealous:
34.16: Do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land, lest they will stray after their idols and they will offer to their gods, and they will invite you and you will eat of their sacrifices;
34.17: And you will take some of their daughters for your sons and you will give your daughters to their sons, and your daughters will go astray after their gods and will seduce your sons after their gods.
34.18: Gods that are molten you shall not make to yourselves.
34.19: Keep the feast of unleavened bread for seven days; you will eat unleavened bread as I ordered you in the time of the month of the flowers, because in the month of Habiba you came out from Egypt.
34.20: Everything opening the womb to me and every firstborn of your cattle of oxen and of sheep.
34.21: And the firstborn of oxen you shall redeem with a lamb, and if you will not redeem it, you shall kill it, and every firstborn of your sons you shall redeem and you shall not appear before me with nothing.
34.22: Six days you shall serve and in the seventh day you shall be rested; in sowing and in harvest you shall be rested.
34.23: And the feast of sevens you shall make to you at the beginning of the harvest of wheat and at the feast of gathering in the outgoing of the year.
34.24: Three times in a year your every memorial shall appear before LORD JEHOVAH, God of Israel.
34.25: Because I shall destroy the nations from before you and I shall enlarge your borders and no man will lust for your land when you go up to appear before LORD JEHOVAH your God three times in a year.
34.26: You shall not sacrifice blood of the sacrifice with leaven, and the sacrifice of the feast of Passover will not remain until the morning.
34.27: The beginning of the harvests of your land you shall bring forth to the house of LORD JEHOVAH your God. You shall not boil a goat in the milk of its mother.
34.28: And LORD JEHOVAH said to Moshe, “Write for yourselves these statements, because I have established these words as a covenant with you and with all Israel.”
34.29: And he was there with LORD JEHOVAH forty days and forty nights; he ate no bread and he drank no water and he wrote on the tablets of stone the words of the covenant, The Ten Statements.
34.30: And when Moshe went down from the mountain of Sinai, two tablets of the Testimony were in the hand of Moshe when he went down from the mountain and Moshe did not know that the skin of his face shone as God spoke with him.
34.31: And Ahron and all Israel saw that the skin of the face of Moshe shone, and they were afraid to approach him.
34.32: And Moshe called them and Ahron and all the Leaders of the Assembly came to him and Moshe spoke with them.
34.33: And then all of the children of Israel approached him and he commanded them everything that LORD JEHOVAH spoke with him in the mountain of Sinai.
34.34: And when Moshe had finished speaking with them he cast a veil over his face.
34.35: And when Moshe was entering before LORD JEHOVAH to speak with Him, he was taking the veil to himself until he would go out, and when he had gone out, he said to those of the house of Israel anything that he was commanded.

35.1: And the children of Israel were looking at the face of Moshe because the skin of the face of Moshe shone; and Moshe was taking the veil from his face when he was entering to speak with Him.
35.2: And Moshe gathered all of the Assembly of the children of Israel and he said to them: “These are the words that LORD JEHOVAH commanded to do.
35.3: Six days you shall do work and the seventh day shall be holy, the Sabbath rest to LORD JEHOVAH; everyone who will do work in it will be killed.
35.4: You shall not kindle fire in all the house of your dwelling in the day of the Sabbath.
35.5: And Moshe said to all of the Assembly of the children of Israel: “This is the word that LORD JEHOVAH commanded to do,
35.6: Take from yourselves an offering to LORD JEHOVAH, every man who has considered in his heart may bring an offering to LORD JEHOVAH, gold and silver and brass,
35.7: And blue fringes, and purple, and dye of scarlet, and fine woven white linen, and hair of goats,
35.8: And skins of rams that are dyed red, and skins of sky blue, and wood of the box tree.
35.9: And oil to give light, and fragrances for oil of anointing, and for incense of sweet spices.
35.10: And stones of beryl, and stones of the setting for the ephod and for the breastplate.
35.11: And all the wise of heart that are among you will come and they will make everything that LORD JEHOVAH commanded;
35.12: The Tabernacle and its curtain, and its covering, and its curtain clasps, and of the boards, and its bars, and its pillars, and its sockets,
35.13: And the ark and its poles and the breastplate and the entrance of the door of the curtain,
35.14: And the table and its poles and all its implements and the bread of the facesPresence,
35.15: And the menorah that gives light in it, and its implements, and its lamps, and oil that gives light in it,
35.16: And the altar of incense and its poles, and oil of anointing and incense of sweet spices, and the curtain of the door of the Tabernacle,
35.17: And the altar of burning, and the base of brass, and its poles, and all its implements, and the basin and its base,
35.18: And the curtain of the courtyard and its pillars and its sockets, and the covering of the doors of the courtyard, and pegs of the Tabernacle, and the pegs of the doors, and their cords,
35.19: And the garments of the ministry to serve in holiness, and the holy garments for Ahron the Priest and the garments for his sons for the priesthood.
35.20: And all of the assembly of the children of Israel went out from before Moshe.
35.21: And every man who consulted with his heart brought something, and every man who considered in his spirit brought an offering to LORD JEHOVAH for the work of the Time Tent and for all its service and for the garments of holiness.
35.22: And men with women, everyone who consulted with his heart were bringing; they brought bracelets and earrings and rings of gold and necklaces and all articles of gold; and every man who set aside an offering to LORD JEHOVAH.
35.23: And every man with whom was found blue and purple and dye of scarlet and fine linen, and hair of goats and skins of rams that are dyed red, and skins of sky blue, brought something.
35.24: Everyone who set aside a gift of silver and of brass brought a gift to LORD JEHOVAH, and everyone with whom was found wood of the box tree brought for all the work of the labor.
35.25: And every woman wise of heart spun with her hands and brought spun blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine white linen.
35.26: And all women who consulted their hearts in wisdom spun hair.
35.27: And Princes brought stones of beryl and stones of the setting for the ephod and for the breastplate;
35.28: And fragrances and oil to enlighten and the oil of anointing and incense of sweet spices.
35.29: Every man and woman who purposed in their hearts to bring all the work that LORD JEHOVAH commanded to do by the hand of Moshe, the children of Israel were bringing a gift to LORD JEHOVAH.
35.30: And Moshe said to the children of Israel: “See that LORD JEHOVAH has called Betsalieil, son of Uri, son of Khur, by name from the tribe of Yehuda.
35.31: And the Spirit of God filled him in wisdom and in understanding and in knowledge and in every work;
35.32: To conceive ideas to make in gold and in silver and in brass
35.33: And in stone cutting to set stones, and in carpentry woodworking, every work of skill.
35.34: And teaching, He put that in his heart, and Elihab, son of Akhisamak from the tribe of Dan.
35.35: Wisdom of Heart filled them to do every work: a carpenter, and a craftsman, and an embroiderer in blue fringes and in purple and in fine white linen and in dye of scarlet and in weaving, workers of every work and planners of ideas.

36.1: And Betsalieil and Elihab and every man wise of heart, to whom LORD JEHOVAH gave wisdom and understanding, worked to know and to do works of the labor of Holiness, according to what LORD JEHOVAH commanded.
36.2: And Moshe called Betsalieil and Elihab and every man wise of heart to whom LORD JEHOVAH gave wisdom in his heart, everyone who purposed in his heart to come to the work to make it:
36.3: And they took from before Moshe every offering that the sons of Israel brought to the work of the Time Tent, to make it, and those bringing to him an offering again, from dawn to dawn.
36.4: And all the wise who were doing the work of Holiness, each man brought from the work that they were doing;
36.5: And they said to Moshe, “The people increase to bring works now for the ministry that LORD JEHOVAH commanded to perform.”
36.6: And Moshe commanded and called Heralds in the camp and they said, “A man or a woman shall make no more works for an offering of Holiness”, and the people were restrained from bringing.
36.7: And the service was enough to make all the work, and there was a surplus.
36.8: And all the wise in heart worked and they made the work of the Tabernacle of ten curtains of fine woven white linen, the blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet, the Cherubim, the work of a craftsman they made them.
36.9: The length of one curtain was twenty and eight cubits, and its width four cubits; the measure of one curtain was the same for all the curtains.
36.10: And they joined five curtains as one and five curtains they joined one to one.
36.11: And they made curtain loops of blue fringes on the edge of one curtain from the side of the selvedge and thus they made in its edge of the curtain from the side of another selvedge.
36.12: Fifty loops they made for one curtain and fifty loops they made on the side of one curtain in another selvedge, bringing together loops, one against another.
36.13: And they made fifty clasps of gold and they joined a curtain, one on one, with a clasp, and it was one Tabernacle.
36.14: And they made a curtain of hair to spread out on the Tabernacle; they made them eleven curtains.
36.15: The length of this curtain was thirty cubits and the width of one curtain, four cubits, one measure for eleven curtains.
36.16: And they joined five curtains as one and six curtains as one.
36.17: And they made loops: fifty loops on the edge of the curtain, from the side of the selvedge, and fifty loops they made on the edge of the curtain in the other selvedge.
36.18: And they made fifty clasps of brass for the packing up of the Tabernacle to be a unit.
36.19: And they made a covering for the Tabernacle: skins of rams that are dyed red and a covering of leather of sky blue from above.
36.20: And they made boards for the Tabernacle of wood of the box tree, standing up.
36.21: Ten cubits, the length of one board, and a cubit and half a cubit, the width of one board.
36.22: Two sockets to one board, and two sockets to one board, upright, one against one, thus they made all the boards of the Tabernacle.
36.23: And they made boards for the Tabernacle; twenty boards of the south wind side:
36.24: And forty sockets of silver they made under the twenty boards; two for side sockets under one board for its two hinges, and two sockets under one board for its two hinges.
36.25: And for another side of the Tabernacle, for the side of the north wind they made twenty boards.
36.26: And forty sockets of silver: two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
36.27: And for the outer skirts of the Tabernacle, from the west they made six boards.
36.28: And two boards they made for the corners of the Tabernacle on their outer skirts.
36.29: And they were doubled from beneath and their tops coupled together on one ring; in this way they made them both for both corners.
36.30: And there were eight boards and their sixteen sockets of silver; two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board.
36.31: And they made bars of wood of the box tree, five for the boards of one side of the Tabernacle.
36.32: And five bars for boards the other side of the Tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the Tabernacle for their outer skirts, from the West.
36.33: And they made the middle bar to pass within the boards from this side to that.
36.34: And the boards they overlaid in gold, and they made their rings of gold a place for the bars, and they overlaid the bars in gold.
36.35: And they made a veil of blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen, its work of Cherubim the work of a craftsman.
36.36: And they made for it four pillars of boxwood and they overlaid them in gold and their capitals were gold, and they made for them four sockets of silver.
36.37: And they made a covering for the door of the Tabernacle: blue fringes and purple and dye of scarlet and fine woven white linen, the work of embroidery;
36.38: Its pillars are five, and their capitals five, and the overlay of their capitals and their overlay of gold and their five sockets of brass.

37.1: And Betsalieil made the ark of wood of the box tree two cubits and a half in its length, and a cubit and a half its width, and a cubit and a half its height:
37.2: And he overlaid it in pure gold from within and from outside and he made for it a crown of gold as a circle.
37.3: And he devised for it four rings of gold on the four corners, two rings on one side and two rings on its other side.
37.4: And he made poles of wood of the box tree and he plated them in gold.
37.5: And he brought the poles into the rings on the sides of the Ark to carry the Ark.
37.6: And he made the Mercy Seat of pure gold, two cubits and a half its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
37.7: And he made two Cherubim of molten gold; he made them at the two sides of the Mercy Seat;
37.8: One Cherub at the side here and the other Cherub at the other side over the Mercy Seat; he made the Cherubim at its two ends.
37.9: And the Cherubim were spreading their wings above and overshadowing with their wings over the Mercy Seat and their faces, one facing the other; the faces of the Cherubim were over the Mercy Seat.
37.10: And he made the table of wood of the box tree, two cubits its length, and a cubit its width, and a cubit and a half its height:
37.11: And he overlaid it in pure gold and he made for it a crown of gold on its edge as a circle.
37.12: And he made for it a border, a hands breadth as a circle, and he made for it a crown of gold on its rim as a circle.
37.13: And he devised for it four rings of gold and put the rings on the four corners on its four feet.
37.14: Against the rim were its rings, the place for the poles to carry the table.
37.15: And he made poles of wood of the box tree and coated them in gold to pick up the table by them.
37.16: And he made the vessels that are upon the table: its flagons and its spoons and its dishes and bowls of pure gold of the drink offering.
37.17: And he made a lamp stand of pure molten gold; he made the Menorah, its base and its branches, and its cups, and its fruits, and its lilies; they were of it:
37.18: And six branches that come out from its sides, three branches of the Menorah from its one side and three branches of the Menorah from its other side:
37.19: Three bowls fastened on one branch and its fruits and the lilies and three cups fastened on one branch and the fruits and lilies in this way in six branches coming out from it, from the lamp stand.
37.20: And on the lamp stand, four bowls joined and the fruits:
37.21: And the lilies, the fruit under two branches of it, and the fruit under two branches of it, and the fruit under two branches of it; in this way for the six branches that come out from the lamp stand.
37.22: And its fruits and the branches of it were all one molten work of pure gold.
37.23: And he made its seven lamps and its snuffers and its dishes of pure gold.
37.24: From a talent of pure gold he made it, it and all its implements.
